brick_analysis.address: Red brick church, near Oxford St

measurements.measurement_reference: Side window approximately 1.5m tall, ~19-20 courses sill to lintel. Church windows are taller than residential standard.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond across entire side elevation. Very consistent coursing maintained over long unbroken wall plane. No decorative bond variations on this elevation.

colour.description: Medium red brick, somewhat more orange-red than the darker residential brick in the area. Some colour variation between courses suggesting different batches or firing temperatures. Lower courses show darker tones from ground splash/moisture. Upper courses lighter and more weathered.

special_features.description: Simple flat arches above the tall side windows
Stone or cast stone window sills (not brick rowlock) — white/light grey

condition.spalling_locations: Upper courses near eave line show some spalling
condition.notes: A significant vertical structural crack/repair is visible running from near the roofline down to the foundation on the side elevation. The crack has been repaired/patched but is clearly visible as a vertical line of contrasting mortar colour. Mortar is weathered throughout — original lime mortar has receded below the brick face in many locations. Some modern repointing with Portland cement visible (lighter grey colour, wider joint fill). Steeple/spire visible above roofline. Building appears to be an active church.

texture_calibration.notes: Church windows are taller than standard residential (est. 1.5m not 1.3m). Long unbroken side wall gives excellent consistency check — courses remain level over full length. Photo taken at slight oblique angle. Structural crack repair provides useful vertical reference.
